# gomail

```go
import "."
```

Package gomail provides a simple interface to compose emails and to mail them efficiently.

More info on Github: https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

func main() {
	m := gomail.NewMessage()
	m.SetHeader("From", "alex@example.com")
	m.SetHeader("To", "bob@example.com", "cora@example.com")
	m.SetAddressHeader("Cc", "dan@example.com", "Dan")
	m.SetHeader("Subject", "Hello!")
	m.SetBody("text/html", "Hello <b>Bob</b> and <i>Cora</i>!")
	m.Attach("/home/Alex/lolcat.jpg")

	d := gomail.NewDialer("smtp.example.com", 587, "user", "123456")

	// Send the email to Bob, Cora and Dan.
	if err := d.DialAndSend(m); err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Daemon)</summary>
<p>

A daemon that listens to a channel and sends all incoming messages.

```go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"time"

	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

func main() {
	ch := make(chan *gomail.Message)

	go func() {
		d := gomail.NewDialer("smtp.example.com", 587, "user", "123456")

		var s gomail.SendCloser
		var err error
		open := false
		for {
			select {
			case m, ok := <-ch:
				if !ok {
					return
				}
				if !open {
					if s, err = d.Dial(); err != nil {
						panic(err)
					}
					open = true
				}
				if err := gomail.Send(s, m); err != nil {
					log.Print(err)
				}
			// Close the connection to the SMTP server if no email was sent in
			// the last 30 seconds.
			case <-time.After(30 * time.Second):
				if open {
					if err := s.Close(); err != nil {
						panic(err)
					}
					open = false
				}
			}
		}
	}()

	// Use the channel in your program to send emails.

	// Close the channel to stop the mail daemon.
	close(ch)
}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Newsletter)</summary>
<p>

Efficiently send a customized newsletter to a list of recipients.

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"log"

	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

func main() {
	// The list of recipients.
	var list []struct {
		Name    string
		Address string
	}

	d := gomail.NewDialer("smtp.example.com", 587, "user", "123456")
	s, err := d.Dial()
	if err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}

	m := gomail.NewMessage()
	for _, r := range list {
		m.SetHeader("From", "no-reply@example.com")
		m.SetAddressHeader("To", r.Address, r.Name)
		m.SetHeader("Subject", "Newsletter #1")
		m.SetBody("text/html", fmt.Sprintf("Hello %s!", r.Name))

		if err := gomail.Send(s, m); err != nil {
			log.Printf("Could not send email to %q: %v", r.Address, err)
		}
		m.Reset()
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (No Auth)</summary>
<p>

Send an email using a local SMTP server.

```go
package main

import (
	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

func main() {
	m := gomail.NewMessage()
	m.SetHeader("From", "from@example.com")
	m.SetHeader("To", "to@example.com")
	m.SetHeader("Subject", "Hello!")
	m.SetBody("text/plain", "Hello!")

	d := gomail.Dialer{Host: "localhost", Port: 587}
	if err := d.DialAndSend(m); err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (No SMTP)</summary>
<p>

Send an email using an API or postfix.

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"io"

	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

func main() {
	m := gomail.NewMessage()
	m.SetHeader("From", "from@example.com")
	m.SetHeader("To", "to@example.com")
	m.SetHeader("Subject", "Hello!")
	m.SetBody("text/plain", "Hello!")

	s := gomail.SendFunc(func(from string, to []string, msg io.WriterTo) error {
		// Implements you email-sending function, for example by calling
		// an API, or running postfix, etc.
		fmt.Println("From:", from)
		fmt.Println("To:", to)
		return nil
	})

	if err := gomail.Send(s, m); err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
}
```

#### Output

```
From: from@example.com
To: [to@example.com]
```

</p>
</details>

## func [Send](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/send.go#L36>)

<a name="Dialer"></a>
## type [Dialer](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/smtp.go#L14-L36>)

A Dialer is a dialer to an SMTP server.

```go
type Dialer struct {
    // Host represents the host of the SMTP server.
    Host string
    // Port represents the port of the SMTP server.
    Port int
    // Username is the username to use to authenticate to the SMTP server.
    Username string
    // Password is the password to use to authenticate to the SMTP server.
    Password string
    // Auth represents the authentication mechanism used to authenticate to the
    // SMTP server.
    Auth smtp.Auth
    // SSL defines whether an SSL connection is used. It should be false in
    // most cases since the authentication mechanism should use the STARTTLS
    // extension instead.
    SSL bool
    // TSLConfig represents the TLS configuration used for the TLS (when the
    // STARTTLS extension is used) or SSL connection.
    TLSConfig *tls.Config
    // LocalName is the hostname sent to the SMTP server with the HELO command.
    // By default, "localhost" is sent.
    LocalName string
}
```

<a name="NewDialer"></a>


```go
func NewPlainDialer(host string, port int, username, password string) *Dialer
```

Deprecated: Use NewDialer instead.

```go
func (d *Dialer) Dial() (SendCloser, error)
```

```go
func (d *Dialer) DialAndSend(m ...*Message) error
```

DialAndSend opens a connection to the SMTP server, sends the given emails and closes the connection.

<a name="Encoding"></a>
## type [Encoding](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L87>)

Encoding represents a MIME encoding scheme like quoted\-printable or base64.

```go
type Encoding string
```

<a name="QuotedPrintable"></a>

```go
const (
    // QuotedPrintable represents the quoted-printable encoding as defined in
    // RFC 2045.
    QuotedPrintable Encoding = "quoted-printable"
    // Base64 represents the base64 encoding as defined in RFC 2045.
    Base64 Encoding = "base64"
    // Unencoded can be used to avoid encoding the body of an email. The headers
    // will still be encoded using quoted-printable encoding.
    Unencoded Encoding = "8bit"
)
```

<a name="FileSetting"></a>
## type [FileSetting](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L252>)

A FileSetting can be used as an argument in Message.Attach or Message.Embed.

```go
type FileSetting func(*file)
```

<a name="Rename"></a>
### func [Rename](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L269>)

```go
func Rename(name string) FileSetting
```

Rename is a file setting to set the name of the attachment if the name is different than the filename on disk.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

var m *gomail.Message

func main() {
	m.Attach("/tmp/0000146.jpg", gomail.Rename("picture.jpg"))
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="SetCopyFunc"></a>
### func [SetCopyFunc](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L280>)

```go
func SetCopyFunc(f func(io.Writer) error) FileSetting
```

SetCopyFunc is a file setting to replace the function that runs when the message is sent. It should copy the content of the file to the io.Writer.

The default copy function opens the file with the given filename, and copy its content to the io.Writer.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"io"

	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

var m *gomail.Message

func main() {
	m.Attach("foo.txt", gomail.SetCopyFunc(func(w io.Writer) error {
		_, err := w.Write([]byte("Content of foo.txt"))
		return err
	}))
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="SetHeader"></a>
### func [SetHeader](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L259>)

```go
func SetHeader(h map[string][]string) FileSetting
```

SetHeader is a file setting to set the MIME header of the message part that contains the file content.

Mandatory headers are automatically added if they are not set when sending the email.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

var m *gomail.Message

func main() {
	h := map[string][]string{"Content-ID": {"<foo@bar.mail>"}}
	m.Attach("foo.jpg", gomail.SetHeader(h))
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Message"></a>
## type [Message](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L12-L21>)

Message represents an email.

```go
type Message struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewMessage"></a>
### func [NewMessage](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L33>)

```go
func NewMessage(settings ...MessageSetting) *Message
```

NewMessage creates a new message. It uses UTF\-8 and quoted\-printable encoding by default.

<a name="Message.AddAlternative"></a>
### func \(\*Message\) [AddAlternative](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L197>)

```go
func (m *Message) AddAlternative(contentType, body string, settings ...PartSetting)
```

AddAlternative adds an alternative part to the message.

It is commonly used to send HTML emails that default to the plain text version for backward compatibility. AddAlternative appends the new part to the end of the message. So the plain text part should be added before the HTML part. See http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MIME#Alternative

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

var m *gomail.Message

func main() {
	m.SetBody("text/plain", "Hello!")
	m.AddAlternative("text/html", "<p>Hello!</p>")
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Message.AddAlternativeWriter"></a>
### func \(\*Message\) [AddAlternativeWriter](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L210>)

```go
func (m *Message) AddAlternativeWriter(contentType string, f func(io.Writer) error, settings ...PartSetting)
```

AddAlternativeWriter adds an alternative part to the message. It can be useful with the text/template or html/template packages.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"html/template"
	"io"

	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

var m *gomail.Message

func main() {
	t := template.Must(template.New("example").Parse("Hello {{.}}!"))
	m.AddAlternativeWriter("text/plain", func(w io.Writer) error {
		return t.Execute(w, "Bob")
	})
}
```

</p>
</details>

```go
import (

var m *gomail.Message

func main() {

}
```

<a name="Message.Embed"></a>
### func \(\*Message\) [Embed](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L320>)

```go
func (m *Message) Embed(filename string, settings ...FileSetting)
```

Embed embeds the images to the email.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

var m *gomail.Message

func main() {
	m.Embed("/tmp/image.jpg")
	m.SetBody("text/html", `<img src="cid:image.jpg" alt="My image" />`)
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Message.FormatAddress"></a>
### func \(\*Message\) [FormatAddress](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L129>)

```go
func (m *Message) FormatAddress(address, name string) string
```

FormatAddress formats an address and a name as a valid RFC 5322 address.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

var m *gomail.Message

func main() {
	m.SetHeader("To", m.FormatAddress("bob@example.com", "Bob"), m.FormatAddress("cora@example.com", "Cora"))
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Message.FormatDate"></a>
### func \(\*Message\) [FormatDate](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L176>)

```go
func (m *Message) FormatDate(date time.Time) string
```

FormatDate formats a date as a valid RFC 5322 date.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"time"

	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

var m *gomail.Message

func main() {
	m.SetHeaders(map[string][]string{
		"X-Date": {m.FormatDate(time.Now())},
	})
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Message.Reset"></a>
### func \(\*Message\) [Reset](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L53>)

```go
func (m *Message) Reset()
```

Reset resets the message so it can be reused. The message keeps its previous settings so it is in the same state that after a call to NewMessage.

<a name="Message.SetBody"></a>

```go
func (m *Message) SetBody(contentType, body string, settings ...PartSetting)
```

SetBody sets the body of the message. It replaces any content previously set by SetBody, AddAlternative or AddAlternativeWriter.

```go
package main

import (
	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

var m *gomail.Message

func main() {
	m.SetBody("text/plain", "Hello!")
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="MessageSetting"></a>
## type [MessageSetting](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L70>)

A MessageSetting can be used as an argument in NewMessage to configure an email.

```go
type MessageSetting func(m *Message)
```

<a name="SetCharset"></a>
### func [SetCharset](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L73>)

```go
func SetCharset(charset string) MessageSetting
```

SetCharset is a message setting to set the charset of the email.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

var m *gomail.Message

func main() {
	m = gomail.NewMessage(gomail.SetCharset("ISO-8859-1"))
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="SetEncoding"></a>
### func [SetEncoding](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L80>)

```go
func SetEncoding(enc Encoding) MessageSetting
```

SetEncoding is a message setting to set the encoding of the email.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

var m *gomail.Message

func main() {
	m = gomail.NewMessage(gomail.SetEncoding(gomail.Base64))
}
```

</p>

<a name="PartSetting"></a>
## type [PartSetting](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L231>)

A PartSetting can be used as an argument in Message.SetBody, Message.AddAlternative or Message.AddAlternativeWriter to configure the part added to a message.

```go
type PartSetting func(*part)
```

<a name="SetPartEncoding"></a>
### func [SetPartEncoding](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/message.go#L235>)

```go
func SetPartEncoding(e Encoding) PartSetting
```

SetPartEncoding sets the encoding of the part added to the message. By default, parts use the same encoding than the message.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"gopkg.in/gomail.v2"
)

var m *gomail.Message

func main() {
	m.SetBody("text/plain", "Hello!", gomail.SetPartEncoding(gomail.Unencoded))
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="SendCloser"></a>
## type [SendCloser](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/send.go#L18-L21>)

SendCloser is the interface that groups the Send and Close methods.

```go
type SendCloser interface {
    Sender
    Close() error
}
```

<a name="SendFunc"></a>
## type [SendFunc](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/send.go#L28>)

A SendFunc is a function that sends emails to the given addresses.

The SendFunc type is an adapter to allow the use of ordinary functions as email senders. If f is a function with the appropriate signature, SendFunc\(f\) is a Sender object that calls f.

```go
type SendFunc func(from string, to []string, msg io.WriterTo) error
```

<a name="SendFunc.Send"></a>
### func \(SendFunc\) [Send](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/send.go#L31>)

```go
func (f SendFunc) Send(from string, to []string, msg io.WriterTo) error
```

Send calls f\(from, to, msg\).

<a name="Sender"></a>
## type [Sender](<https://github.com/go-gomail/gomail/blob/master/send.go#L13-L15>)

Sender is the interface that wraps the Send method.

Send sends an email to the given addresses.

```go
type Sender interface {
    Send(from string, to []string, msg io.WriterTo) error
}
```
